longboat-key.summary: Longboat Key is a 10-mile barrier island between Sarasota Bay and the Gulf of Mexico — and one of Florida's quietest luxury resort communities. The island is overwhelmingly residential, favoring low-rise condominiums and Gulf-front and bayfront homes over commercial development, and beach access is the main event: miles of soft, uncrowded sand define daily life, kept serene in part by deliberately limited public parking and very little retail. At its heart, The Resort at Longboat Key Club anchors island life with 45 holes of championship golf — the Gulf-side Islandside course and the bayfront Harbourside layouts — alongside the deep-water Longboat Key Club Moorings, a 291-slip marina that berths vessels up to 150 feet. The 2024 arrival of The St. Regis Longboat Key Resort, set on 18 beachfront acres with 800 feet of private Gulf frontage, has further sharpened the island's standing among discerning buyers. The result is a market defined by privacy, water, and a slower, more residential rhythm than anywhere else on the Sarasota coast.

video.title: This Week on Longboat Key
video.caption: Past 7 days · 12 homes sold · median $1,187,500 · 69 median days on market.
video.description: Weekly Longboat Key, Florida real estate market update from Ryan Adamson, The Adamson Group: in the past 7 days, 12 homes sold with a median price of $1,187,500 and a median 69 days on market.
